data.title: NETSTREIT reports Q1 results
data.description:
• NETSTREIT press release (NTST): Q1 FFO of $0.32.
  • Completed $239.0 Million of Gross Investment Activity at 7.5% Blended Cash Yield for First Quarter.
  • Increases 2026 AFFO Per Share Guidance to a New Range of $1.36 to $1.39.
  •  $314.3 Million of Gross Forward Equity Sales via February Follow-On and ATM in First Quarter.
  • Increases 2026 Net Investment Guidance Range to $550 Million to $650 Million 

2026 GUIDANCE

  • The Company is increasing its full-year 2026 AFFO per share guidance range to $1.36 to $1.39 from $1.35 to $1.39 and increasing net investment activity guidance to $550.0 million to $650.0 million from $350.0 million to $450.0 million. The Company continues to expect cash G&A to range between $16.0 million and $17.0 million (exclusive of transaction costs and severance payments). In addition, the company's AFFO per share guidance range now includes $0.03 to $0.06 per share of estimated dilution (vs. the previous $0.015 to $0.03 per share) due to the impact of the company's outstanding forward equity calculated in accordance with the treasury stock method.

data.title: Swvl Holdings regains Nasdaq compliance
data.description:
• Swvl (SWVL) has regained compliance with Nasdaq listing rules on minimum equity and net income.
  • The company now meets $2.5M equity and $500K net income requirements.
  • Swvl reported $2.9M in stockholders’ equity and $1.3M in net income for FY2025.
  • The company had previously been out of compliance since October 31, 2025, including market value requirements.

data.title: Coinbase-Bybit eyes tokenized U.S. stocks as volume hits $2.87B
data.description:
Coinbase Global ($COIN) is reportedly collaborating with Bybit on a major push into asset tokenization, marking a potential step toward bringing traditional U.S. equities on-chain for global users. 

The discussions, which are still ongoing, focus on tokenized stocks, custody solutions, and distribution infrastructure for both U.S. public shares and pre-IPO assets.

As per CoinDesk's sources, the arrangement does not involve any equity stake acquisition or a formal deal enabling Bybit’s direct entry into the U.S. market. Instead, Bybit’s planned U.S. expansion is expected to run via a separate entity led by former co-CEO Helen Liu.

A local partner is expected to handle licensing and compliance, while Bybit may contribute technology, product infrastructure, and liquidity.

The latest report suggests that both companies were already discussing wider cooperation, mainly around regulated market access and tokenized asset distribution. 

This move comes as tokenized equities are growing fast, with trading volumes reaching about ~$2.87B in March, rising over 80% in just 30 days. At the same time, the total number of holders crossed 200K, reported RWA.xyz.

Industry analysts believe such partnerships could significantly expand global access to U.S. assets via a single digital platform. Meanwhile, Intercontinental Exchange ($ICE), owner of the NYSE, has also moved into the space by taking a stake in crypto exchange OKX (OKX-USD).

Following the news, $COIN rose ~3% to $211.68.

data.title: AXT plans public stock offering; shares down
data.description:
• AXT (AXTI) announced plans to offer shares of common stock in a public offering.
  • Underwriters will have a 30-day option to purchase up to 15% additional shares (overallotment option).
  • Proceeds will primarily support subsidiary Beijing Tongmei Xtal Technology in expanding indium phosphide substrate production.
  • Funds will also be used for R&D of new or improved products.
  • Additional uses include working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • AXTI shares fell 7% post-market.
 
 

Your Premium trial has ended

data.title: Broadcom shares slip after nine-session winning streak
data.description:
Broadcom (AVGO) shares snapped nine straight sessions of gains on Monday, as the stock was 1.7% lower at $399.46.

The semiconductor and infrastructure software solutions company gained nearly 22% in the preceding nine sessions. So far this year, the stock has added over 16%, compared to the 4% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index (SP500).

AVGO is up 24% over the past one month.

Most recently, Broadcom entered into a three-year partnership with Meta Platforms (META) to help the company develop its artificial intelligence chips and deploy them into data centers.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, AVGO has a Hold rating with a score of 3.5 out of 5. The company received A+ in the prospect of profitability and growth, while it received F in the valuation factor.

Seeking Alpha analysts are positive and see the stock as a Buy.

Turning to the Wall Street, 44 analysts have given the stock a Buy or above rating. Three gave the stock hold recommendation, while none gave Sell rating to AVGO.

 

“Despite a premium valuation, Broadcom offers significant upside as earnings scale rapidly, with potential for substantial re-rating as AI demand continues accelerating through 2027,” pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

data.title: CNB Financial Non-GAAP EPS of $0.87, revenue of $83.32M
data.description:
• CNB Financial press release (CCNE): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.87.
  • Revenue of $83.32M.
    • Loans - Excluding $78.3 million of syndicated loan balances, loans were $6.4 billion as of March 31, 2026. Organic loans decreased for the quarter by $67.3 million, or 1.41% (5.73% annualized), compared to December 31, 2025.1 The decrease in organic loans was driven primarily by an increased level of prepayments in certain larger Commercial Real Estate (“CRE”) loans.
    • Deposits - At March 31, 2026, total deposits were $7.1 billion. Including $89.9 million in deposits classified as held for sale, organic deposit growth for the quarter totaled $115.0 million, or 1.62% (6.55% annualized), compared to December 31, 2025.1
    • Net Interest Margin - Net interest margin was 3.83% for the three months ended March 31, 2026, compared to 3.84% for the three months ended December 31, 2025. Net interest margin on a fully tax-equivalent basis, a non-GAAP measure, was 3.84% and 3.84%, for the three months ended March 31, 2026 and December 31, 2025, respectively.1 Included in net interest margin on a fully tax-equivalent basis was $3.0 million and $3.2 million of purchase accounting loan accretion for the three months ended March 31, 2026 and December 31, 2025, respectively.
    • Credit Quality - Total nonperforming assets were approximately $49.2 million, or 0.58% of total assets, as of March 31, 2026, compared to $42.2 million, or 0.50% of total assets, as of December 31, 2025.

data.title: Prairiesky Royalty GAAP EPS of C$0.24, revenue of C$133.8M
data.description:
• Prairiesky Royalty press release (PREKF): Q1 GAAP EPS of C$0.24.
  • Revenue of C$133.8M (+4.4% Y/Y).

data.title: Spruce Biosciences announces proposed public offering of common stock, pre-funded warrants
data.description:
• Spruce Biosciences (SPRB) on Monday said it has commenced an underwritten public offering of shares of its common stock or pre-funded warrants to purchase shares of its common stock in lieu thereof.
  • All of the securities are being offered by Spruce Biosciences.
  • In addition, Spruce Biosciences expects to grant the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase additional shares of its common stock in an amount of up to 15% of the aggregate number of shares sold in the offering at the public offering price, less underwriting discounts and commissions. 
  • SPRB -3.03% after hours to $67.0.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Intel drops as many semiconductor stocks make Monday gains
data.description:
Intel (INTC) shares had declined 4% by afternoon trading on Monday as an array of semiconductor stocks were poised to close in the green.

However, Intel has been on a recent tear, as shares have climbed more than 40% over the past month.

MaxLinear had surged more than 20% by Monday afternoon. Shares of the systems-on-chip solutions company have nearly doubled over the past month. It is slated to release its first quarter 2026 financial results on Thursday. A consensus estimate calls for adjusted earnings per share of $0.18 on revenue of $134.6M. That would be an impressive turnaround from one year prior, when it reported an EPS of ($0.05) on revenue of $96M.

Credo (CRDO) and Marvell (MRVL) had both jumped 6%. It was reported over the weekend that Marvell may work with Google (GOOG)(GOOGL) on developing future artificial intelligence chips. 

The sector was mostly up. The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index (SOX) had edged up 0.3%. This contrasted with the major indices, as the Dow Jones (DJI), S&P 500 (SP500), and the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) had all dipped less than 0.5%.

Texas Instruments (TXN), Onsemi (ON), Microchip Technology (MCHP), Analog Devices (ADI), and Monolithic Power Systems (MPWR) had all edged up between 1% and 3%.

In contrast, most of the big-name semiconductor stocks were slightly down on Monday. Micron (MU) had declined 1.5%, Nvidia (NVDA) inched down 0.2%, Broadcom (AVGO) had dropped about 2%, and AMD (AMD) and Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) were both down 1%.

Arm (ARM) had gained 4%, and Qualcomm (QCOM) had inched up about 1%.  

Alpha and Omega Semiconductor (AOSL) had surged 18%.

data.title: SmartFinancial targets $4 EPS run rate by Q4 2026 as it projects flat Q2 NIM before a second-half lift
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: SmartFinancial (SMBK) Q1 2026

Management View

  • "It was a great start to the year" as the company reported tangible book value of "$27.33 per share, up from $26.86 at year-end," along with "operating earnings of $13.7 million or $0.81 per diluted share" and "total operating revenue" of "$53.8 million" (CEO William Carroll).
  • "We continue to execute on outstanding growth on both sides of the balance sheet," including "14% annualized growth in loans" and "7% annualized growth in core deposits," while credit metrics remained low with "only 25 basis points in nonperforming assets" (CEO William Carroll).
  • "We had a senior team addition with a new Director of Private Banking and Wealth Management" and management described wealth and investments as having "steadily grown" and becoming "a great business driver for us" (CEO William Carroll).
  • "Nonbrokered deposits" increased "by $95 million" and the company "paid down the remaining $52 million in brokered deposits" as "interest-bearing deposits declined by 19 basis points to 2.60%" (CFO Ronald Gorczynski).
  • "Net interest income for the quarter was $45.9 million" and "net interest margin also improved by 10 basis points to 3.48%" (CFO Ronald Gorczynski).
  • "Our provision expense for the quarter was $4.1 million" as the bank "updated" its CECL allowance model; the allowance for credit losses rose to "$44 million, representing 0.97% of total loans" (CFO Ronald Gorczynski).
  • "We feel very confident in our ability now to move through the 1% and 12% ROA and ROE thresholds as we look into 2026" and management reiterated the internal "4x4 challenge of hitting a $4 EPS run rate by the fourth quarter of 2026" (CEO William Carroll).
  • "We've elevated Cynthia Cain to our Chief Operating Officer role" and management said she "will be tasked on aligning all of our operational and tech initiatives" (CEO William Carroll).

Outlook

  • "We anticipate that our margin will stabilize and remain relatively flat for the second quarter before increasing slightly in the second half of the year" (CFO Ronald Gorczynski).
  • "For the second quarter, noninterest income is projected to be approximately $7.8 million and noninterest expense is expected to be in the range of $34 million to $34.5 million" (CFO Ronald Gorczynski).
  • "Salary and benefit expenses are anticipated to range from $20.5 million to $21 million" due to "the full quarter effects of our merit increases and new hires" (CFO Ronald Gorczynski).
  • Compared with last quarter’s framing of "projecting our first quarter 2026 margin in the 3.4% to 3.45% range," Q1 management shifted to a second-quarter comment of "relatively flat" NIM before a "second half" increase (CFO Ronald Gorczynski).

Financial Results

  • The company reported "operating earnings of $13.7 million or $0.81 per diluted share" on "total operating revenue" of "$53.8 million" (CEO William Carroll).
  • Profitability and spread performance were supported by "net interest margin" of "3.48%" and "net interest income" of "$45.9 million" (CFO Ronald Gorczynski).
  • Credit provisioning was driven by modeling and growth items as "provision expense" was "$4.1 million," including "$926,000 attributable to an increase in our unfunded commitments liability" (CFO Ronald Gorczynski).
  • Fee income mix shifted as "operating noninterest income was $7.9 million" with "higher investment services fees" offsetting "lower mortgage banking and capital markets revenue" that was "lower primarily due to seasonality" (CFO Ronald Gorczynski).
  • Expense items included "operating noninterest expenses" of "$32.9 million" and an FDIC-related benefit where the company "reduced our FDIC insurance accrual of $275,000" but "expect this expense to return to normal levels in future periods" (CFO Ronald Gorczynski).
  • Capital and liquidity metrics included "loan deposit ratio of 87%," a "consolidated TCE ratio" of "8%," and "total risk-based capital" of "12.7%" (CFO Ronald Gorczynski).

Q&A

  • Brett Rabatin, StoneX: asked whether aggressive competitor pricing (especially in Tennessee) could affect growth sustainability; CEO Carroll said, "we could have had a lot more" loan growth but the bank is "turning away some deals" due to "unreasonable rate competition," while Chief Credit Officer Rhett Jordan said the "pipeline itself... continues to backfill at a pretty consistent pace."
  • Brett Rabatin, StoneX: asked about loan-to-deposit guardrails and funding; CFO Gorczynski said the bank has been "hovering around the 86%, 87%" loan-to-deposit ratio and "not afraid to go up to 90, 90-plus," adding that deposit generation has been strong and "a lot of it's been money market generated."
  • Russell Gunther, Stephens: asked if deposit costs can keep falling if the Fed stays on pause; CFO Gorczynski said, "we're pretty neutral at this point in time" and "we will still see a little bit of rate movement upward... very few basis points quarter-over-quarter."
  • Russell Gunther, Stephens: asked whether competition is increasing on loans and deposits; CEO Carroll said, "competition is ramping up" and added, "It's on both sides."
  • Russell Gunther, Stephens: asked whether production yields are holding; CFO Gorczynski said the bank is renewing "88%" of loans coming up and renewals are "coming in about 120 basis points higher" (with management describing rates as broadly consistent with current levels).
  • Catherine Mealor, KBW: asked what rate forecast supports the NIM outlook; CFO Gorczynski said, "We're not assuming up or down at this point in time" and later confirmed, "Correct" when asked about a flat-rate environment.
  • Catherine Mealor, KBW: asked whether the company still views ~5% expense growth as appropriate amid recruiting; CFO Gorczynski said the bank expects to stay in "a tight band between $34.5 million to $35 million" and flagged that "variable comp... could change some of this" with stronger production.
  • Stephen Scouten, Piper Sandler: asked the biggest risk to second-half NIM upside; CFO Gorczynski said it is "all going to be in the funding cost," and CEO Carroll pointed to "competitive pressure on... funding rates" as the key swing factor.
  • Stephen Scouten, Piper Sandler: asked if money market growth was driven by promotions; management said, "we didn't have any rate promos" and CFO Gorczynski added, "we prefer selling money markets than CDs" (CEO Carroll; CFO Gorczynski).
  • Stephen Scouten, Piper Sandler: asked about Nashville expansion and the wealth hire; CEO Carroll said Nashville has "really good energy" and "a heck of an upside for us" and indicated more hiring and potentially additional office capacity.
  • Stephen Moss, Raymond James: asked about pipeline mix (construction/CRE) and portfolio balance; Chief Credit Officer Jordan said the portfolio mix is "maintaining a pretty steady pace" with only "a percentage point or 2" shifting quarter to quarter.
  • Stephen Moss, Raymond James: asked about de novo and M&A; CEO Carroll said "No, not really" on major de novo expansion beyond Columbus and added on M&A, "it'd take a unicorn to probably get us to move" (CEO Carroll).

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were slightly negative to neutral on competitive dynamics and margin durability, repeatedly pressing on pricing and funding (e.g., "competition is ramping up" framing and questions on "biggest risk" to NIM) (Russell Gunther; Stephen Scouten).
  • Management tone was slightly positive and execution-focused, emphasizing discipline and confidence (e.g., "we're turning away some deals" and "we feel very confident" on return thresholds) while acknowledging competition and funding-cost risk (CEO Carroll; CFO Gorczynski).
  • Versus last quarter’s emphasis on "the best year in our company's history" and NIM trajectory toward "3.50%, plus or minus" by year-end, the current call kept a similar confidence level but leaned more into competitive pressure and the mechanics of deposit mix and funding costs (CEO Carroll; CFO Gorczynski).

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Management kept the same strategic emphasis on organic growth and discipline, reiterating the internal goal to reach "a $4 EPS run rate by the fourth quarter of 2026" and continuing to de-emphasize M&A (CEO Carroll).
  • The prepared narrative shifted from Q4’s expansion announcement into Columbus, Georgia to Q1 execution updates (management said it is "excited" about building Columbus quickly, while also highlighting a wealth leadership hire and COO promotion) (CEO Carroll).
  • Funding and margin discussion evolved from Q4’s explicit Q1 NIM range of "3.4% to 3.45%" to Q1’s guidance that NIM should be "relatively flat" in Q2 before "increasing slightly" later in the year, with Q&A more concentrated on competitive pricing and funding costs (CFO Gorczynski).
  • Credit commentary moved from Q4’s "transition to a new allowance model" expectation to Q1’s completed model update and resulting allowance and provision detail (CFO Gorczynski).

Risks and Concerns

  • Management said it is seeing "unreasonable rate competition" and "competition is ramping up" on both loans and deposits, contributing to the bank "turning away some deals" to protect returns (CEO Carroll).
  • Margin risk was characterized as funding-driven, with management stating the back-half NIM outcome is "all going to be in the funding cost" amid mix shifts and competitive deposit pricing (CFO Gorczynski).
  • Provisioning was elevated by methodology changes as the bank "updated" its CECL model, increasing the allowance to "0.97% of total loans" and lifting unfunded commitment reserves (CFO Gorczynski).

Final Takeaway

Management described Q1 as a strong start to 2026 built on balance-sheet growth, improved margin, and disciplined pricing amid intensifying competition. The company highlighted leadership moves in operations and wealth management, reiterated its internal target of a $4 EPS run rate by Q4 2026, and guided to a relatively flat net interest margin in Q2 with a modest second-half lift, while consistently pointing to funding costs and competitive deposit dynamics as the primary swing factors.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
